project_profile.deployment_model: Python package installed via pip/setuptools, invoked as library API from user scripts or via build.py for building/testing

trust_boundaries.untrusted_side: User-provided file paths, build scripts, kernel binaries

files.reason: Executes arbitrary bash scripts via subprocess.run, compiles user-provided source files, loads dynamic libraries
Generates C++ code that embeds kernel names, file paths, and parameters - code injection risk
Dynamically loads user-provided .so modules via importlib, uses ctypes for library loading
Executes msprof profiling command, reads profiling output files, manages multiprocessing
orchestrates tiling_func and get_kernel_from_binary workflows that execute compiled code
Processes user-provided configuration parameters including file paths, tensor data
Modifies kernel source files based on user-provided configurations
Security validation functions for file paths and permissions - defense but also potential bypass
Uses ctypes.CDLL to load runtime libraries, reads environment variables
Utility functions for autotune, reads JSON files and kernel source files
Logging functionality, reads environment variable for log level
Global context management, stores kernel parameters and paths
Build script that executes subprocess commands for building and testing
Package setup, reads environment variable for version

entry_points.justification: User calls mskl.compile(build_script, launch_src_file) from their script. build_script path is user-controlled and executed as bash script via subprocess.run
entry_points.description: API entry point that executes arbitrary bash scripts for compilation

entry_points.justification: User calls mskl.compile_executable(build_script, src_file) from their script. build_script path is user-controlled and executed as bash script
entry_points.description: API entry point for compiling executables via user-provided build scripts

entry_points.justification: User calls mskl.tiling_func() with op_type, inputs, outputs, lib_path, etc. Parameters influence generated code and compiled modules
entry_points.description: API entry point for tiling function invocation

entry_points.justification: User calls mskl.get_kernel_from_binary(kernel_binary_file) with path to kernel binary. Path is validated but user-controlled
entry_points.description: API entry point for loading kernel from binary file

entry_points.justification: User creates Launcher(config) which generates C++ glue code. Config contains user-provided kernel_src_file and kernel_name
Decorator @autotune(configs) receives user-provided config list. Configs are used to modify kernel source files
Decorator @autotune_v2(configs) receives user-provided config list and executes msprof profiling
CLI tool invoked by user with command line arguments. Executes subprocess commands for build/test
Reads LD_PRELOAD environment variable to check for preloaded libraries. Environment variables are typically controlled by deployment/admin
entry_points.description: Environment variable read for security check

entry_points.justification: Reads ASCEND_HOME_PATH environment variable to load libmspti.so. Path is deployment-controlled
entry_points.description: Environment variable read for library loading

entry_points.justification: Reads ASCEND_HOME_PATH environment variable. Required for CANN installation path
entry_points.description: Environment variable read for CANN path

entry_points.justification: Reads MSKL_LOG_LEVEL environment variable for log configuration
entry_points.description: Environment variable read for logging configuration

attack_surfaces: Bash script execution via mskl.compile() and mskl.compile_executable() - build_script parameter
Dynamic library loading via importlib.util - compiled .so modules
Code generation - C++ glue code with embedded kernel names and paths
File path parameters - kernel_src_file, kernel_binary_file, lib_path
Configuration parameters - op_type, inputs, outputs, attrs
Auto-tune config modification - kernel source file rewriting
